subject: Cloud Computing
title: Regional Cloud Champions
description: A quick win: understand Regional Cloud Champions.

hook.text: Not every country trusts American cloud providers. Regional champions serve local markets worldwide.

buildup.text: OVHcloud dominates in France. Yandex Cloud serves Russia. Tencent and Huawei compete in China.

discovery.text: Data sovereignty laws force some customers to use local providers. Their data must stay in-country.

twist.text: Regional providers can't match the big three in features. But compliance requirements make them essential.

climax.text: The cloud market isn't one global contest. It's dozens of local battles with different winners in each.

punchline.text: Global cloud has local rules. Local players know them best.

quiz.question: Why do regional cloud providers survive against the global giants?
options: Data sovereignty laws often require local data storage and processing
Regional providers always have better technology than AWS
Global cloud providers refuse to serve small countries
